title: leontodon hispidus subsp. danubialis
intro: Leontodon hispidus subsp. danubialis, also known as the Danube hairy hawk's beard, is a subspecies of the dandelion-like plant Leontodon hispidus, native to Eastern Europe. The medicinal properties of this plant are attributed to its leaves, roots, and flowers, which are rich in flavonoids, alkaloids, and other bioactive compounds. It is used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory infections, and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The plant can be prepared as tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional folk medicine to promote digestive health, boost the immune system, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
